business.headline: $53 billion wiped out: Middle East war shakes global airline industryAs the war enters its fourth week, airlines are struggling to cope with a sharp rise in jet fuel prices, which have surged significantly in a short span of time. The impact is already visible for passengers, with ticket prices expected to rise in the coming months as airlines try to protect already thin profit margins.

Oil shock deepens as West Asia war escalates. Is a global recession coming?The world is already feeling the heat as the West Asia conflict enters its fourth week, pushing global crude oil prices higher. In this article, we speak to experts to understand whether this shock could tip the world toward a recession.

Govt nod to hike commercial LPG allocation to 50% as domestic output improvesThe additional allocation will be subject to commercial establishments registering with oil companies and applying for a piped natural gas connection, according to a letter written by the oil secretary to states.

Flight tickets likely to get costlier from April 1. Here's whyCivil Aviation Minister Ram Mohan Naidu said that the impact of the ongoing Iran war on the aviation sector is likely to be visible from April 1, when the Air Turbine Fuel (ATF) prices will be revised.

Raymond's Chairman Gautam Singhania injured as his speedboat overturns in MaldivesGautam Singhania sustained minor injuries after a speedboat capsized in the Maldives. Two Indian nationals remain missing following the accident.

May lead to spike in fares: Airlines oppose 60% free seat ruleA new government directive on free seat allocation has triggered sharp opposition from airlines, who warn it could push up fares, hurt affordability and reshape how tickets are priced across India's aviation sector.

Why bank lockers are hard to get and what you can do insteadThis is the third part of Lock & Key, a series that looks at how India's bank locker system works, the risks involved, and what customers need to know. After examining safety and insurance in the first two parts, this story focusses on a new problem – access.

World economy in serious trouble, India vulnerable: Raghuram Rajan on oil crisisThe former RBI Govenor said a 15–20% global energy disruption would require sharp demand cuts, pushing oil to $150–$200 per barrel, risking severe economic stress if the conflict continues another month.

Oil could hit $150-$200 per barrel if Iran war drags on: Raghuram RajanFormer RBI Governor Raghuram Rajan warned that if the US-Israeli war against Iran continues beyond a month, oil prices could surge to $150-$200 per barrel, potentially triggering a global recession.

Premium petrol prices up by Rs 2, no change in normal petrol, diesel ratesIndian Oil Corporation Limited (IOCL) and Hindustan Petroleum Corporation Limited (HPCL) have increased premium petrol prices by ₹2 per litre, with IOCL XP95 now priced at ₹101.80 per litre. The hike affects premium petrol used mostly in performance vehicles, while regular petrol prices remain unchanged.
Sensex ends 326 points higher, Nifty above 23,100; HDFC Bank falls 2%The S&P BSE Sensex added 325.72 points to end at 74,532.96, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 112.35 points to end at 23,114.50.

Zomato hikes food delivery platform fee to Rs 14.90 from Rs 12.50 per orderThe platform fee now stands at Rs 14.90 per order on a pre-GST basis, up from Rs 12.50 earlier. This marks another round of increase, with the last hike having taken place in September 2025.

Flipkart CFO Sriram Venkataraman to step down ahead of planned IPOVenkataraman joined Flipkart in 2015 and has been part of the company's leadership team through a period of growth and restructuring. During his tenure, he helped strengthen the company's financial operations.

Premium petrol prices hiked by Rs 2 per litre; regular fuel unchangedThe hike applies to high-octane fuels such as Indian Oil's XP95 and Hindustan Petroleum's Power petrol, based on dealer inputs from select cities.

Is the 20-minute neighbourhood the future of urban living?Urban planners across global cities increasingly speak of the "20-minute neighbourhood", a model where residents can access most aspects of daily life within roughly twenty minutes.

Buying insurance online? 8 in 10 users say it can lead to problemsA nationwide survey has found that more than 80% of users faced issues such as hidden charges, difficulty with cancelling policies, forced data sharing and misleading pricing while using online insurance platforms.

Gold prices today: Why are rates rising after a steep fall and should you buy?Gold prices are edging higher after a sharp fall, but the trend remains uncertain as global cues and a strong dollar continue to drive volatility.

HDFC Bank share price extends fall, down 2% after sharp sell-offThe stock slipped 2.19% to Rs 782.15 on the BSE at 10:35 am, adding to losses after it had plunged more than 5% in the previous session, marking one of its sharpest declines in recent months.

Rupee hits all-time low, slips past Rs 93 mark against US dollarThe rupee weakened to Rs 93.15 against the US dollar in early trade, extending recent losses amid rising geopolitical tensions and a surge in crude oil prices.

Sensex jumps 700 points: Why is stock market rising today? 3 key reasonsThe rebound comes as easing oil prices, positive global cues, strong buying in IT stocks, and broad-based buying after Thursday's sharp fall lifted investor sentiment.

entertainment.headline: Ranveer Singh gets 'Babbar Sher' cheers during outing with Deepika Padukone. Video Ranveer Singh stepped out with Deepika Padukone amid the success of Dhurandhar: The Revenge, drawing loud cheers from fans during their outing.

Get info from better sources: Vishal Dadlani on Dhurandhar 2 demonetisation track Vishal Dadlani seemingly took a dig at Dhurandhar: The Revenge over its depiction of demonetisation, sharing posts that questioned its real-world impact.

Me with the Power Couple: RGV's Dhurandhar photo-op with Aditya Dhar, Yami Gautam Ram Gopal Varma met Aditya Dhar and Yami Gautam, calling them a "power couple" while continuing to praise Dhurandhar: The Revenge.

Dhurandhar 2: Watch Ranveer Singh's ghost transformation BTS clip Celebrity make-up artist Preetisheel Singh shared a BTS video revealing Ranveer Singh's intricate "ghost born from shadows" transformation in Dhurandhar: The Revenge.

Hits like a war drum: Rishab Shetty praises Dhurandhar 2 amid Kantara mimicry row Kannada actor-director Rishab Shetty praised Dhurandhar: The Revenge amid the Kantara mimicry row, calling it a powerful and immersive cinematic experience. Telugu star Venkatesh said it takes immense vision to bring Dhurandhar to life.

Nani dances with family, Mahesh Babu's daughter cheers at Anirudh's Hyderabad gig At Anirudh Ravichander's Hyderabad concert, Nani attended with his wife Anjana, while Mahesh Babu's daughter Sitara Ghattamaneni was seen interacting with fans. Visuals from the show have emerged online.

Karan Johar says he has Dhurandhar FOMO; check out the reason Karan Johar expressed "Dhurandhar FOMO" as he is yet to watch Dhurandhar: The Revenge. In a social media post, the filmmaker revealed why he hasn't been able to watch the film so far. Read here to know.

Peaky Blinders The Immortal Man review: A dark, gritty farewell for Tommy ShelbyRelease Date:Fri Mar 20, 2026 Peaky Blinders: The Immortal Man reframes war through a deeply personal lens, following Tommy Shelby as he grapples with power, legacy, and inner conflict in a world shaped by ongoing violence.

Ayushmann Khurrana to Anupam Kher, celebs praise Ranveer Singh's Dhurandhar 2 Dhurandhar: The Revenge has received widespread reactions from Bollywood celebrities, highlighting its strong storytelling and performances. Actors like Ayushmann Khurrana, Anupam Kher, Arjun Kapoor, Rajkummar Rao, Rakul Preet Singh and Riteish Deshmukh have praised the film.

Deepika Padukone's quiet support for Ranveer Singh: Why social media can't let it be The internet is losing it over Deepika Padukone's silence on Ranveer Singh's Dhurandhar 2. But since when did a happy marriage require an Instagram post? The trolling reflects the classic double standard: Demanding wives be public cheerleaders while ignoring their right to a private life.

BTS' comeback album ARIRANG review: Seven voices, one evolving identity BTS returns with ARIRANG, blending tradition and innovation to showcase their evolving identity after a long hiatus and military service. This album marks a new chapter where each member's unique voice contributes to a fluid, dynamic collective sound.

Meet Pari Pandher: The breakout star behind Jasleen Rangi in Dhurandhar 2 The emotional sibling reunion scene between Jaskirat Rangi and Jasleen Rangi in Dhurandhar 2 has won hearts, marking a strong Bollywood debut for Punjabi actor Pari Pandher.

Singer Justin Timberlake's 2024 arrest bodycam footage released Justin Timberlake's 2024 DWI arrest footage from New York's Hamptons has been released despite his legal efforts to keep it private. The Grammy winner pleaded guilty to a lesser charge and completed community service.

Aditya Dhar's Dhurandhar 2 politics explained: Anti-terror, not anti-Pakistan Aditya Dhar doesn't make it easy to watch Dhurandhar: The Revenge. He lets you experience the discomfort, the silence that haunts and the screams that linger longer than you want. But at the centre of it all is India's clear policy: anti-terror, not anti-Pakistan. Consider this your spoiler warning.

Justin Bieber, Usher in tense exchange at 98th Oscars after-party: Report The 98th Academy Awards after-party saw a reported clash between Justin Bieber and Usher. Their long-time mentor-protege bond now appears strained, with neither addressing the incident publicly.

Dhurandhar's wildest twist belongs to Jameel Jamali, and fans weren't ready for it Spoiler alert: Dhurandhar: The Revenge flips the script with a quiet but powerful reveal – Jameel Jamali as the true mastermind. The twist reshapes the story, turning it into a game of control, patience, and hidden power.

Shah Rukh Khan wishes 'love and light' on Eid, fans say 'we got our eidi' Shah Rukh Khan extended heartfelt Eid greetings to his fans, wishing them peace, joy and prosperity on this festive occasion. He is gearing up for his upcoming film King, set for release in December 2026.

Dhurandhar 2 box office Day 3: Rs 500 crore worldwide for Ranveer's juggernaut Dhurandhar: The Revenge has breached the Rs 500 crore mark worldwide in just three days. This festive box office surge was always expected from the Aditya Dhar directorial, and it is set to grow even bigger on Sunday. Check the detailed collection report here.

Rashmika Mandanna-Vijay Deverakonda's cosy Thailand honeymoon pic melts internet Newlyweds Rashmika Mandanna and Vijay Deverakonda are winning hearts with a romantic photo from their Thailand honeymoon. The couple got married in February 2026.

general news.headline: Iran claims it intercepted, struck US's F-15 jet near Hormuz islandIran claimed it intercepted and struck a US F-15 near Hormuz after it allegedly entered its airspace, amid escalating US-Iran-Israel war.

Trump threatens Iran energy sites over Hormuz, Tehran warns of strikes in GulfIran has warned it is ready to respond. Parliament Speaker Mohammad Baqer Qalibaf warned that if Iran's infrastructure is targeted, then key facilities across the region could be "irreversibly destroyed."

The ace up Iran's sleeve? What is a dirty bomb and will Tehran use one?With uranium enriched to 60%, it is not possible to build a nuclear bomb, but a radiological dispersal device can still be made. Such devices can be used to disperse radiological dust and smoke over a wide area. If Iran can make it, it could make Israel very uncomfortable.

New import sources, export markets: PM reviews fuel, food security at key meetPM Narendra Modi chaired a high-level meeting to review India's energy and fertiliser supply amid the ongoing West Asia conflict, focusing on uninterrupted supply and stable logistics.

I was offered lavender marriage. A closer look revealed the dark realityI was proposed lavender marriage twice within five months. Such moments seemingly unfold daily on matrimonial apps as queer people turn to this arrangement for social camouflage. A woman is offered a life of opulence abroad, but the emotional cost is huge and legal guardrails near-zero.

Woman, lover murder mother-in-law, stage it as robbery; CCTV exposes plotA 36-year-old woman plotted to murder her mother-in-law with the help of her 21-year-old lover. The murder plot was exposed by CCTV installed inside the house.

Deepika Padukone's quiet support for Ranveer Singh: Why social media can't let it beThe internet is losing it over Deepika Padukone's silence on Ranveer Singh's Dhurandhar 2. But since when did a happy marriage require an Instagram post? The trolling reflects the classic double standard: Demanding wives be public cheerleaders while ignoring their right to a private life.

Live: Iran says Strait of Hormuz is open to all except those who violate sovereigntyUS-Israel-Iran War Live Updates: The conflict in the Middle East witnessed an escalation on Sunday (IST) after a missile struck the city of Dimona, which hosts Israel's main nuclear research facility. This came as Iran said it was targeting Israel's nuclear programme in retaliation for an earlier strike on its nuclear enrichment facility in Natanz. Arad, another city in southern Israel, also came under a missile strike. The two incidents resulted in injuries to more than 100 people, including children. Israel has denied responsibility for the strike on Natanz. Hours after the strikes on Dimona and Arad, the Israeli military said it was conducting a fresh airstrike on Tehran. In another development, US President Donald Trump threatened Iran with strikes on its power plants if the Strait of Hormuz is not reopened "within 48 hours." Follow IndiaToday.in for live updates on the Middle East conflict.

Hits like a war drum: Rishab Shetty praises Dhurandhar 2 amid Kantara mimicry rowKannada actor-director Rishab Shetty praised Dhurandhar: The Revenge amid the Kantara mimicry row, calling it a powerful and immersive cinematic experience. Telugu star Venkatesh said it takes immense vision to bring Dhurandhar to life.

Russian oil tanker bound for China arrives in India; first of seven vesselsOil tanker Aqua Titan carrying Russian crude arrived at the New Mangaluru port on Saturday evening. The re-routing of such vessels comes after Indian refiners stepped up purchases of Russian oil following the 30-day waiver by the US.

Drug addict kills nurse wife with anaesthesia overdose months after marriageThe couple had married just four months ago, and investigators say the crime followed disputes over his concealed drug addiction.

Trump makes good on his 'ICE agents at US airports' threat over funding impasseIn a post on Truth Social, Donald Trump said ICE personnel would assist Transportation Security Administration (TSA) officers who have continued to work despite uncertainty over funding.

More rains coming: Back-to-back weather systems to hit India next weekA feeble western disturbance is set to influence weather conditions on Monday, March 23, particularly across Delhi-NCR, Haryana, Punjab, north Rajasthan, and Chandigarh.

Blindfolded pot game, barber act: Quirky campaign moments grab attention in BengalFrom blindfolded games and barber acts to fish-themed messaging, candidates in West Bengal are turning to unconventional, highly visual campaign tactics to connect with voters, even as the ruling Trinamool Congress pushes a wider anti-incumbency reset before April 23 polling.
The death of 'lived-in homes' in IndiaIn the race to design the "perfect" home, are we losing what made homes feel like ours?

1 killed, 17 injured as ropeway collapses at Chhattisgarh temple during Navratri rushA ropeway accident at a hilltop temple in Chhattisgarh's Mahasamund district triggered panic among devotees, leaving one woman dead and 17 injured, with several shifted to Raipur as authorities probe possible maintenance lapses.

Ground report: The war is loud. Beirut's kindness is almost a whisperIn a city reshaped by war and displacement, survival in Beirut now rests on quiet acts of care. Each morning, strangers show up — not with answers, but with what little they can carry.
Question Time: Weekly News QuizThink you read the headlines this week? Let's test that!

Hitman hired to kill son murders woman, loots Rs 10 lakh; caught after 5 yearsA murder-for-hire plot took a shocking turn when the hired killer turned on his own employer. What began as a family feud over property ended in a cold-blooded killing — and a five-year chase.

All except enemy ships can pass Hormuz: Iran after Trump's 48-hour ultimatumThe statement comes hours after US President Donald Trump threatened to target Iranian power plants if the waterway does not "fully open" within 48 hours.
What date and time will Nasa'a Artemis-II mission launch to the Moon?The mission aims to test critical systems needed for future lunar landings, including those planned under Artemis III.

If US targets our nukes, we'll bomb Delhi, Mumbai: Ex-Pak envoy's bizarre remarkA controversial remark by a former Pakistani diplomat has triggered concern amid rising regional tensions, with references to India drawing sharp reactions as geopolitical fault lines appear to widen further.

Woman alleges sexual exploitation, filming, blackmail for cash on marriage promiseThe woman alleged that the accused secretly recorded private photos and videos and used them to blackmail her. The woman claimed she was forced to pay around Rs 1.8 lakh.

Nvidia CEO says engineers should be evaluated on basis of how many AI tokens they useNvidia CEO Jensen Huang says the rise of AI is changing software engineering so much that companies may soon judge engineers by how many AI tokens they use instead of just how much code they write. He believes access to AI compute will become a key productivity tool, and engineers who use more AI effectively will deliver better results.

sports.headline: PCB will take action against players opting out of PSL for IPL: Mohsin Naqvi PCB chief Mohsin Naqvi has warned that players who leave the PSL for the IPL will face action under league rules, citing Corbin Bosch's one-year ban last year as a precedent.

Watch: Suresh Raina rolls back years, hits Noor Ahmed for six in front of MS Dhoni IPL 2026: Suresh Raina thrilled Chepauk with a vintage six off Noor Ahmed. The moment, with MS Dhoni watching on, sent fans into nostalgia mode.

Rishabh Pant with most pressure in IPL 2026: Du Plessis urges LSG captain to deliver IPL 2026: Faf du Plessis believes Rishabh Pant will face the most pressure in IPL 2026. The LSG captain will need to deliver after a disappointing 2025 season.

MS Dhoni steals spotlight at CSK reunion, honours Sanju Samson, Shivam Dube MS Dhoni stole the spotlight at CSK's 'Roar 26' event in Chennai, as former stars, including Suresh Raina and Matthew Hayden, reunited at the MA Chidambaram Stadium on Sunday. The former captain also honoured World Cup-winning stars Sanju Samson, Shivam Dube and Ayush Mhatre ahead of IPL 2026.

Thatrom Thookrom: Sanju Samson thrills CSK fans with Tamil punchline IPL 2026: Sanju Samson thrilled CSK fans with a Tamil punchline at the ROAR 2026 event. His words and presence sparked massive cheers at Chepauk.

Australia Women shrug off India setback to hammer West Indies in T20I series AUSW vs WIW, 2nd T20I: Australia Women sealed the T20I series against West Indies with a 17-run win in the second match in Kingstown, bouncing back strongly after last month's 2-1 home series defeat to India and taking an unassailable 2-0 lead.

PSL continues to lose players as Shanaka set to join IPL as injury replacement IPL 2026: Dasun Shanaka is set to leave PSL side Lahore Qalandars to join Rajasthan Royals as Sam Curran's replacement. His move adds to the growing list of players choosing IPL over PSL.

KKR fans furious as bowling coach Bravo spotted at CSK event during injury crisis IPL 2026: KKR fans erupted in anger after bowling coach Dwayne Bravo was spotted at CSK's event, raising questions about divided loyalties amid a mounting injury crisis at the franchise.

South Africa hold back New Zealand in Wellington to force T20I series decider South Africa beat New Zealand by 19 runs in the fourth T20I in Wellington to level the five-match series at 2-2 and force a decider, after posting 164 for five and bowling the hosts out for 145 in 18.5 overs.

Was Rishabh Pant leaving LSG? Sanjiv Goenka breaks silence, laughs off rumours IPL 2026: Lucknow Super Giants owner Sanjiv Goenka, speaking to India Today's sister channel SportsTak, backed Rishabh Pant to deliver and admitted the team's mistakes last season cost them a top-four finish.

Shreyanka Patil: Inspired by Rahul Dravid's Adelaide 233 and Virat Kohli, I made my comeback Shreyanka Patil, in an exclusive interview with India Today Digital, said that Rahul Dravid's inspirational 233 in Adelaide and Virat Kohli's remarkable innings at the venue inspired her comeback.

No crowds at PSL 2026 as US-Israel-Iran war triggers oil crisis in PakistanPSL 2026 will go ahead as scheduled but with major changes, as Pakistan's fuel crisis and security concerns have forced the PCB to shift the tournament to just Karachi and Lahore, with matches to be played behind closed doors.

Virat Kohli laughs off claims he sought charter flights to London for IPL 2026IPL 2026: Virat Kohli laughed off claims he sought charter flights to London during IPL 2026, responding to social media rumours ahead of Royal Challengers Bengaluru's season opener in Bengaluru.

Lucknow Super Giants make big call to help accommodate Rishabh Pant in IPL 2026IPL 2026: Rishabh Pant is expected to be LSG's new No.3 while Nicholas Pooran gets demoted to the middle-order despite being one of their standout players last season.

Pakistan fined for poor T20 WC 2026: Kirsten says he isn't shocked about newsGary Kirsten said that he was sad to hear the news that the Pakistan players were being fined for their performance in the T20 World Cup 2026. Kirtsen, however, admitted that he wasn't surprised to hear about the move from PCB.

Gavaskar stands his ground, calls for IPL-owned teams to stop paying Pak playersSunil Gavaskar has called on Indian-owned franchises to stop paying Pakistani players, while hitting back at accusations of hypocrisy following his controversial column on the matter.

Premier League title race on hold as Manchester City, Arsenal eye Carabao Cup winWith the Premier League title race put on hold for the moment, Arsenal and Manchester City will meet in the final of the Carabao Cup 2025-26. With silverware up for grabs, which team willk seize the chance to gain the edge and the title.

Ishan Kishan gets fiery send off from own teammate during SRH intra-squad gameSRH camp sees a heated moment as Ishan Kishan gets a fiery send-off from teammate Zeeshan Ansari during an intra-squad match ahead of IPL 2026. Kishan has been named as the interim captain for the first few games for SRH.

Will Sanju Samson be CSK's wicketkeeper in IPL 2026? Ashwin's post suggests otherwiseIPL 2026: Ashwin's post showing Sanju Samson practising high catches in the deep with no keeping gloves has sparked speculation over his role at with the Chennai Super Kings ahead of the new season.

Shreyanka, stronger and wiser, fights past pain to reignite T20 World Cup flameIn an exclusive interview with India Today Digital, Shreyanka Patil opened up on battling one of the toughest phases of her career, with multiple injuries leaving her full of doubt. Having recently made her India comeback on the Australia tour, Shreyanka now hopes to help India chase their maiden Women's T20 World Cup title.

Chelsea thrashed by Everton, Liverpool's top 4 hopes dented as Brighton win 2-1Liverpool and Chelsea stumble in the Premier League top 4 race after their losses to Brighton and Everton respectively. The Reds lost 1-2 at the Amex stadium, while Chelsea were thrashed 3-0 by Everton.

IPL teams should deduct player's money if he is unable to fulfill role: R AshwinR Ashwin has suggested that IPL teams should be allowed to cut money from the players' wages if they're unable to fulfill their role for the side. Ashwin used the example of Cameron Green and KKR to drive home the point.

Sabalenka shocked as Miami Open considered cancelling her match for AlcarazWorld No. 1 Aryna Sabalenka was surprised when Miami Open organisers considered cancelling her match against Ann Li, but she chose to play, securing a 7‑6(5), 6‑4 win.

Watch: Rohit Sharma grooves to Divine, enjoys MI fan fest with daughter SamairaIPL 2026: Rohit Sharma danced with daughter Samaira at Mumbai Indians' fan fest. The video went viral as MI gear up for IPL 2026.

Watch: Babar Azam shows off cooking skills on Eid amid injury controversyPakistan batter Babar Azam delighted his fans on Eid‑ul‑Fitr by sharing a video of himself cooking traditional festive dishes at home.

IPL 2026: Abhishek buries T20 World Cup woes, hits quickfire 94 for SRH intra-squadIPL 2026: Abhishek Sharma smashed a quickfire 94 in SRH's intra-squad clash ahead of IPL 2026. The knock signalled his return to form after a mixed T20 World Cup campaign.

KKR's bowling fear factor is gone, oppositions won't worry anymore: R AshwinIPL 2026: R Ashwin believes KKR's bowling attack has lost the fear factor it once carried, with the mystery around Varun Chakravarthy and Sunil Narine no longer as effective.

Wimbledon steps into new era, to introduce video review technology from 2026Wimbledon confirmed the introduction of video review technology from the 2026 edition. Players will be able to challenge umpire decisions without any limit on reviews.

Not Sanju Samson, CSK should stick with Ruturaj Gaikwad as captain: Robin UthappaIPL 2026: Robin Uthappa backed Ruturaj Gaikwad to continue as CSK captain for IPL 2026. He stressed the need for patience despite Sanju Samson's arrival and growing leadership debate.

Anaya Bangar undergoes gender change surgery, thanks father for supportAnaya Bangar shared that she has successfully undergone gender-affirming surgery in Thailand. She credited her father Sanjay Bangar and family for their support through the emotional journey.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
